meta.originalTitle: Skyscraper Farming 摩天大楼种植

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 14–26, which are based on Reading Passage 2 below.

Today's environmental scientists are in no doubt that the world's resources of fertile soil are rapidly deteriorating, and that new land for agriculture is becoming ever more sparse. Intensive farming, urbanisation, desertification and sea-level rises are all putting growing pressure on the planet's agricultural land and therefore on food supplies. Currently 24 per cent of the world's 11.5 billion hectares of cultivated land has already undergone human-induced soil degradation, particularly through erosion, according to a recent study by the UK Government Office for Science.

The global population is expected to exceed nine billion by 2050 — up a third from today's level — and studies suggest that food production will have to go up by 70 per cent if we are to feed all of those new mouths. This means that scientists will have to develop new ways of growing crops if we are to avoid a humanitarian crisis. Indeed, UN Food and Agriculture Organization figures suggest that the number of under-nourished people is already growing, and with escalating climate change, crop yields in many areas have been projected to decline.

With this in mind, some scientists and agricultural experts are advocating an innovative alternative to traditional farming whereby skyscrapers packed with shelf-based systems for growing vegetables on each storey — known as 'vertical farms' — could hold the key to revolutionising agriculture. Columbia University professor Dickson Despommier claims that vertical farming could boost crop yields many times over. A single 20-storey vertical farm could theoretically feed 50 000 people. And if the theory translates into reality as proposed, 160 skyscraper-sized vertical farms could feed the entire population of New York City, while 180 would be needed for London, 289 for Cairo and 302 for Kolkata.

It's a compelling vision, and one that has already been put into practice in Asia — albeit on a smaller scale. 'But there are problems, such as initial investment and operating costs that are too great,' says a spokesman for Japan's Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. Nevertheless, Tokyo-based mushroom producer Hokuto Corporation is a model example of how a vertical farm can be profitable. With 28 vertical mushroom farms operating across the country, it produces some 68 000 tonnes of mushrooms annually. 'Vertical mushroom farms have more advantages than ground-level farms,’ says Hokuto's Ted Yamanoko, who highlights the relative cost-effectiveness of his organisation's farming practices together with reduced emissions of greenhouse gases.

And the impact of vertical farms could extend beyond feeding established urban populations. Despommier sees them as being capable of helping centres of displaced persons — such as refugee camps — in much the same way that Mobile Army Surgical Hospital (MASH) units are deployed in emergencies. ‘Developing an emergency-response system for crop production inside specially constructed modular and highly transportable greenhouses would allow for humanitarian interventions,' he says. 'If you have three or four storeys of food already growing someplace, they could become mobile units that could be picked up by helicopters and dropped into the middle of a crisis zone.'

But it isn't only about increasing food production. Despommier is concerned about the harm that farming has done to the world's landscape over a relatively short time span, particularly the elimination of hardwood forests. 'Farming is only 12 000 years old,' he points out. 'Vertical farming could allow us for the first time to feed everyone on Earth and still return land to its original ecological function.' Natalie Jeremijenko of New York University agrees. Reducing the land needed for food production could enrich biodiversity, which in turn can raise the productivity of conventional farms. Furthermore, vertical farming could dramatically cut fossil-fuel use and reduce geopolitical tensions in regions where poor farming conditions drive conflict and malnutrition.
Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–ix, in boxes 14–19 on your answer sheet.

children.label: i Potential production capabilities of vertical farms
ii Opposition to new ideas about food production
iii A successful application of vertical-farming technology
iv The potential to provide urgent relief
v The original inspiration for vertical farming
vi Various environmental benefits of vertical farming
vii An increasing problem for farmers worldwide
viii A return to traditional farming methods
ix A rising demand for food

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 1. 段落标题匹配（Questions 14–19：Choose the correct heading）
explanationSection.text:
答案：vii（An increasing problem for farmers worldwide，全球农民日益严重的问题）
解析：Paragraph A 聚焦 “耕地退化、新耕地稀少” 等农业难题，核心是 “全球农业问题加剧”，与标题 vii 一致，因此选 vii。
答案：ix（A rising demand for food，粮食需求上升）
解析：Paragraph B 围绕 “人口增长导致粮食需求增加 70%” 展开，核心是 “需求上升”，与标题 ix 一致，因此选 ix。
答案：i（Potential production capabilities of vertical farms，垂直农场的潜在产量）
解析：Paragraph C 给出垂直农场的产量估算（20 层养活 5 万人、各城市所需数量），核心是 “产量潜力”，与标题 i 一致，因此选 i。
答案：iii（A successful application of vertical-farming technology，垂直农场技术的成功应用）
解析：Paragraph D 以日本北都公司为例，说明垂直农场的盈利和运营，核心是 “成功案例”，与标题 iii 一致，因此选 iii。
答案：iv（The potential to provide urgent relief，提供紧急援助的潜力）
解析：Paragraph E 强调垂直农场可作为 “应急救援单元”，核心是 “紧急援助”，与标题 iv 一致，因此选 iv。
答案：vi（Various environmental benefits of vertical farming，垂直农场的多种环境效益）
解析：Paragraph F 列举 “恢复生态、减少化石燃料、缓解冲突” 等环境与社会效益，核心是 “环境益处”，与标题 vi 一致，因此选 vi。

items.text:
（1）题目 14：Paragraph A
答案：vii（An increasing problem for farmers worldwide，全球农民日益严重的问题）
解析：Paragraph A 聚焦 “耕地退化、新耕地稀少” 等农业难题，核心是 “全球农业问题加剧”，与标题 vii 一致，因此选 vii。

（2）题目 15：Paragraph B
答案：ix（A rising demand for food，粮食需求上升）
解析：Paragraph B 围绕 “人口增长导致粮食需求增加 70%” 展开，核心是 “需求上升”，与标题 ix 一致，因此选 ix。

（3）题目 16：Paragraph C
答案：i（Potential production capabilities of vertical farms，垂直农场的潜在产量）
解析：Paragraph C 给出垂直农场的产量估算（20 层养活 5 万人、各城市所需数量），核心是 “产量潜力”，与标题 i 一致，因此选 i。

（4）题目 17：Paragraph D
答案：iii（A successful application of vertical-farming technology，垂直农场技术的成功应用）
解析：Paragraph D 以日本北都公司为例，说明垂直农场的盈利和运营，核心是 “成功案例”，与标题 iii 一致，因此选 iii。

（5）题目 18：Paragraph E
答案：iv（The potential to provide urgent relief，提供紧急援助的潜力）
解析：Paragraph E 强调垂直农场可作为 “应急救援单元”，核心是 “紧急援助”，与标题 iv 一致，因此选 iv。

（6）题目 19：Paragraph F
答案：vi（Various environmental benefits of vertical farming，垂直农场的多种环境效益）
解析：Paragraph F 列举 “恢复生态、减少化石燃料、缓解冲突” 等环境与社会效益，核心是 “环境益处”，与标题 vi 一致，因此选 vi。

children.text: from the passage for each answer.
A UK Government study found that
is a significant factor contributing to worldwide levels of soil degradation.
Disadvantages of vertical-farming projects include the expense of setting them up, as well as their high
could potentially be used to take vertical-farming facilities to areas where there is a critical food shortage.

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 2. 句子填空（Questions 20–22：NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：英国政府研究发现，\___\___\___\_是导致全球土壤退化的重要因素。
答案：erosion（水土流失）
解析：定位 Paragraph A 中 “24 per cent of the world’s 11.5 billion hectares of cultivated land has already undergone human-induced soil degradation, particularly through erosion”，明确提到 “水土流失” 是主因，因此答案为 “erosion”。
题目翻译：垂直农场项目的劣势包括建设成本高和高_\___\___\___。
答案：operating costs（运营成本）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “initial investment and operating costs that are too great”，明确提到 “运营成本高”，因此答案为 “operating costs”。
题目翻译：\___\___\___\_可能用于将垂直农场设施运往粮食极度短缺的地区。
答案：helicopters（直升机）
解析：定位 Paragraph E 中 “they could become mobile units that could be picked up by helicopters and dropped into the middle of a crisis zone”，明确提到 “直升机” 运输，因此答案为 “helicopters”。

items.text:
（1）题目 20：A UK Government study found that \___\___\____ is a significant factor contributing to worldwide levels of soil degradation.
题目翻译：英国政府研究发现，\___\___\___\_是导致全球土壤退化的重要因素。
答案：erosion（水土流失）
解析：定位 Paragraph A 中 “24 per cent of the world’s 11.5 billion hectares of cultivated land has already undergone human-induced soil degradation, particularly through erosion”，明确提到 “水土流失” 是主因，因此答案为 “erosion”。

（2）题目 21：Disadvantages of vertical-farming projects include the expense of setting them up, as well as their high \___\___\___\_.
题目翻译：垂直农场项目的劣势包括建设成本高和高_\___\___\___。
答案：operating costs（运营成本）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “initial investment and operating costs that are too great”，明确提到 “运营成本高”，因此答案为 “operating costs”。

（3）题目 22：\___\___\____ could potentially be used to take vertical-farming facilities to areas where there is a critical food shortage.
题目翻译：\___\___\___\_可能用于将垂直农场设施运往粮食极度短缺的地区。
答案：helicopters（直升机）
解析：定位 Paragraph E 中 “they could become mobile units that could be picked up by helicopters and dropped into the middle of a crisis zone”，明确提到 “直升机” 运输，因此答案为 “helicopters”。

children.text: Look at the following statements (Questions 23–26) and the list of people below.
Match each statement with the correct person, A, B or C.
NB You may use any letter more than once.
Vertical farming can have financial benefits.
Traditional farming has had a negative effect on the natural world.
Vertical farming could dramatically increase world food production.
Traditional farms may benefit from wider use of vertical farming.

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 3. 人物 - 观点匹配（Questions 23–26：Match each statement with the correct person）
explanationSection.text:
答案：B（Ted Yamanoko，泰德・山子）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “Ted Yamanoko... highlights the relative cost-effectiveness of his organisation’s farming practices”，“cost-effectiveness” 对应 “financial benefits”，因此选 B。
答案：A（Dickson Despommier，迪克森・德斯波米耶）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “Despommier is concerned about the harm that farming has done to the world’s landscape over a relatively short time span, particularly the elimination of hardwood forests”，明确提到传统农业破坏景观，因此选 A。
答案：A（Dickson Despommier，迪克森・德斯波米耶）
解析：定位 Paragraph C 中 “Despommier claims that vertical farming could boost crop yields many times over... a single 20-storey vertical farm could theoretically feed 50 000 people”，明确提到产量大幅提升，因此选 A。
答案：C（Natalie Jeremijenko，娜塔莉・杰里米延科）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “Natalie Jeremijenko... Reducing the land needed for food production could enrich biodiversity, which in turn can raise the productivity of conventional farms”，明确提到传统农场可因生物多样性提升受益，因此选 C。

items.text:
（1）题目 23：Vertical farming can have financial benefits.（垂直农场可带来经济收益）
答案：B（Ted Yamanoko，泰德・山子）
解析：定位 Paragraph D 中 “Ted Yamanoko... highlights the relative cost-effectiveness of his organisation’s farming practices”，“cost-effectiveness” 对应 “financial benefits”，因此选 B。

（2）题目 24：Traditional farming has had a negative effect on the natural world.（传统农业对自然世界有负面影响）
答案：A（Dickson Despommier，迪克森・德斯波米耶）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “Despommier is concerned about the harm that farming has done to the world’s landscape over a relatively short time span, particularly the elimination of hardwood forests”，明确提到传统农业破坏景观，因此选 A。

（3）题目 25：Vertical farming could dramatically increase world food production.（垂直农场可大幅提高全球粮食产量）
答案：A（Dickson Despommier，迪克森・德斯波米耶）
解析：定位 Paragraph C 中 “Despommier claims that vertical farming could boost crop yields many times over... a single 20-storey vertical farm could theoretically feed 50 000 people”，明确提到产量大幅提升，因此选 A。

（4）题目 26：Traditional farms may benefit from wider use of vertical farming.（传统农场可能从垂直农场的广泛应用中受益）
答案：C（Natalie Jeremijenko，娜塔莉・杰里米延科）
解析：定位 Paragraph F 中 “Natalie Jeremijenko... Reducing the land needed for food production could enrich biodiversity, which in turn can raise the productivity of conventional farms”，明确提到传统农场可因生物多样性提升受益，因此选 C。

options.label: i Potential production capabilities of vertical farms
ii Opposition to new ideas about food production
iii A successful application of vertical-farming technology
iv The potential to provide urgent relief
v The original inspiration for vertical farming
vi Various environmental benefits of vertical farming
vii An increasing problem for farmers worldwide
viii A return to traditional farming methods
ix A rising demand for food
